kicker: INSTITUTIONS · WHERE THE HIGH-RISK MONEY IS
lede: IMSS alone carries 799.6 billion MXN in Critical or High-flagged contracts since 2018 — more than the next four institutions combined. Its direct-award rate is 81 percent, triple the OECD ceiling. ISSSTE, IMSS-BIENESTAR, CONAGUA, and the defunct INSABI — dissolved in 2023 but still holding 3,560 contracts on the books — fill out the top five. INSABI's 43 percent high-risk ratio is the worst of any federal agency above the 2,000-contract floor.
lede_none: IMSS alone carries 799.6 billion MXN in Critical or High-flagged contracts since 2018 — more than the next four institutions combined. Its direct-award rate is 81 percent, triple the OECD ceiling.

champions.headline: Five institutions worth modeling on
champions.sub: These agencies rank highest on openness, price fairness and vendor diversity. They prove it is possible.

redFlags.headline: Five institutions that failed the test
redFlags.sub: Bottom-ranked agencies. Each one scores below 40 on the five pillars — a verdict of systemic capture risk.
